findings.location: source introduction (before モジュール一覧)
findings.description: The introduction lists three key benefits of using the Doma adapter that are not captured in any knowledge file section: (1) dynamic SQL building at runtime like Nablarch; (2) 2waySQL means no SQL rewriting needed and SQL files can be executed directly in SQL tools; (3) the Transactional interceptor limits transaction management to only designated actions, reducing unnecessary transaction control processing and improving performance. This is technology selection guidance that AI needs to answer 'why/when to use the Doma adapter' questions.
findings.source_evidence: 「Nablarchと同じように、実行時に動的にSQL文を構築できる」「2waySQLなので、NablarchのようにSQL文を書き換える必要がなく、SQLツール等でそのまま実行できる」「Transactionalインターセプタで指定したアクションのみトランザクション管理対象にできるため、不要なトランザクション制御処理を削減でき、パフォーマンスの向上が期待できる」

findings.description: The knowledge file code ends with `insert(project));` (two closing parentheses before the semicolon), but the source code ends with `insert(project);` (only one closing parenthesis before the semicolon). The knowledge file silently altered the source code.

Same parenthesis discrepancy as in separate-transaction: the knowledge file renders `DomaDaoRepository.get(ProjectDao.class, CustomConfig.class).insert(project));` but the source has `DomaDaoRepository.get(ProjectDao.class, CustomConfig.class).insert(project);`.

Missing hints for annotations and types used in the code examples within this section: `@Select`, `SelectType`, `@Dependent`, `@Named`. All four appear in the source code examples for the Dao interface and ItemReader class.
findings.source_evidence: `@Select(strategy = SelectType.RETURN)`, `@Dependent`, `@Named` in source code examples for the lazy-load section

findings.description: Missing hints for `LocalTransactionDataSource` and `LocalTransactionManager`, which are key classes used in the CustomConfig constructor and referenced in the implementation guidance.
findings.source_evidence: `localTransactionDataSource = new LocalTransactionDataSource(...)` and `localTransactionManager = new LocalTransactionManager(localTransaction)` in the CustomConfig class example
